summary: Gate-relevant behavior adequately covered. safeReaddir's entries/ENOENT/ENOTDIR/re-throw and buildEvidenceItem's collision/defaults/values/carry-through are each asserted, with the two behaviorally-meaningful branches (ENOTDIR→[] and explicit-kind-wins) backed by pre-fix-failing assertions. The classifySeniorAxisEvidence workaround removal is exercised end-to-end via real preparePullRequest but is a behavioral no-op given the buildEvidenceItem fix, so needs no isolated assertion. All 19 tests pass. No untested meaningful branch.

findings.detail: GER-E2E-005's comment references classifySeniorAxisEvidence but its assertion inspects gate:common_judgment_spine (fed by classifyJudgmentEvidence); the senior-axis path runs end-to-end but its emitted items are not directly asserted. Not a real gap — the removal is a behavioral no-op once buildEvidenceItem is fixed; no test can isolate it.

judgment_delta: Initial concern: e2e test claims to validate classifySeniorAxisEvidence removal but asserts on a node fed by classifyJudgmentEvidence. Final: pass — the removal is provably a no-op once buildEvidenceItem's explicit-kind-wins fix is in place (no test can isolate it), meaningful behavior lives in buildEvidenceItem asserted both in unit and e2e, pre-fix-failing assertions exist for both new branches; the node mismatch is low-severity comment nuance, not a regression gap.
